title: psephellus araxinus
intro: Psephellus araxinus, also known as the Armenian scabious, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Caucasus region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving skin conditions, as well as possessing antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. Psephellus araxinus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and flowers are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
